steps.title: Identify the structure
steps.content: The sentence reads: "...hangs [a] 41.1-by-37.6-inch oil ______ which was created in 1910 by American artist Lyonel Feininger." The blank names the painting ("Uprising") and must be punctuated to introduce the relative clause "which was created in 1910."

steps.title: Rule: a nonrestrictive "which" clause must be preceded by a comma
steps.content:
The painting title "Uprising" is the specific noun the clause describes, so the clause is nonrestrictive and requires a comma before "which." Per-choice diagnosis:

A. "painting Uprising," — places the comma after the title, immediately before "which." Correct.

B. "painting Uprising" — omits the comma before "which," violating the nonrestrictive clause rule. Incorrect.

C. "painting, Uprising" — puts a comma between "painting" and "Uprising," interrupting the noun phrase. "Uprising," is left without the required comma before "which." Incorrect.

D. "painting. Uprising." — creates a sentence fragment ("Uprising. which was created...") by using a period after "Uprising." Incorrect.

A is correct.
